unique_mechanism: Hand-digitised stitch maps — every design is redrawn stitch-by-stitch by a human digitiser instead of auto-converted, so fine lines and small text stay crisp

B_gift_giver.dominant_desire: Give a gift that makes the recipient feel deeply known and loved — proof of how well you know them
B_gift_giver.core_problem: Generic personalised gifts (mugs, prints) feel low-effort; buyers fear blurry, cheap-looking embroidery from print-on-demand shops
B_gift_giver.positioning: The gift that makes them cry — heirloom-grade embroidery of the things only you two share
avatar_language: I cried when I opened it
she said it was the best gift she's ever gotten
you can tell it wasn't mass-produced

D_aesthetic_buyer.dominant_desire: Wear pieces that look quiet-luxury custom, not fast-fashion — embroidery as identity
D_aesthetic_buyer.core_problem: Mass-market 'custom' hoodies all use the same fonts and clipart; they look cheap on camera
D_aesthetic_buyer.positioning: Quiet-luxury embroidery — hand-digitised, no clipart, the piece people ask 'where did you get that?'
avatar_language: doesn't look like an Etsy font
